context:
CREATE TABLE customers (
    cust_id   INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    name      TEXT NOT NULL,
    email     TEXT NOT NULL,
    region    TEXT NOT NULL,
    plan      TEXT NOT NULL,
    joined    DATE NOT NULL,
    rep       TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE products (
    sku       TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    product   TEXT NOT NULL,
    cat       TEXT NOT NULL,
    price     INTEGER NOT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE orders (
    oid        INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    cust_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES customers(cust_id),
    sku        TEXT    NOT NULL REFERENCES products(sku),
    dt         DATE    NOT NULL,
    qty        INTEGER NOT NULL,
    unit_price INTEGER NOT NULL,
    discount   REAL    NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    revenue    INTEGER NOT NULL,
    refunded   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
);

COLUMN DESCRIPTIONS:

customers:
  cust_id : Unique integer identifier for each customer.
  name    : Full name of the customer.
  email   : Email address of the customer.
  region  : Geographic sales region the customer belongs to.
             Allowed values: 'North', 'South', 'East', 'West'
  plan    : Subscription tier the customer is on.
             Allowed values: 'free', 'starter', 'pro', 'enterprise'
  joined  : ISO-8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) when the customer first signed up.
  rep     : Full name of the sales representative assigned to this customer.
             NULL if the customer is self-serve (no sales rep).

products:
  sku     : Alphanumeric stock-keeping unit code uniquely identifying the product.
  product : Descriptive name of the product.
  cat     : High-level product category.
             Allowed values: 'Software', 'Hardware', 'Services', 'Support'
  price   : Catalogue list price stored in US cents (integer).
             Example: 29900 means $299.00.

orders:
  oid        : Unique integer identifier for each order.
  cust_id    : Foreign key referencing customers(cust_id).
  sku        : Foreign key referencing products(sku).
  dt         : ISO-8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) the order was placed.
  qty        : Integer number of units purchased in this order.
  unit_price : Actual price per unit in US cents at time of order.
  discount   : Discount fraction applied to this order, stored as a decimal between
               0.0 and 1.0. Example: 0.15 means 15% off.
  revenue    : Net revenue collected, stored in US cents.
               Computed as: ROUND(qty * unit_price * (1 - discount))
  refunded   : Refund flag stored as an integer: 0 = not refunded, 1 = refunded.
